question.text: Which of the following factors affects BOTH call and put option premiums in the same direction?

answer.text: Volatility of the underlying asset — higher volatility increases both call and put premiums
Price of underlying — rising prices increase both
Strike price — rising strike increases both
Interest rates — rising rates increase both

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Higher volatility increases premiums for both calls and puts because greater price uncertainty raises the probability of the option becoming ITM. **The Trap:** Underlying price affects them oppositely (up: call rises, put falls). Strike price affects them oppositely. Interest rates affect them oppositely (up: call rises, put falls). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers the same five determinants of option pricing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Volatility is the single most important factor for option traders — buying options before expected high-volatility events and selling after them is a common strategy.
